title: `bober chat hub` surface with scoped /priority and /decide
description: Register a hub team (data) so `bober chat hub` routes to the hub memory namespace, and add additive /priority and /decide slash commands to the chat session that delegate to the Sprint 3-4 hub judge and return a ranked summary while writing priority.md. Pre-existing slash commands are left unchanged.

successCriteria.description: A hub team is registered as data such that `bober chat hub` starts a chat session whose memory namespace resolves to the hub team's namespace.
Inside a hub chat session, the /priority slash command returns a ranked summary of the collected findings (rank and title per line) using the hub judge.
Inside a hub chat session, /decide X vs Y returns the decision-scoped ranking limited to findings relevant to X or Y.
The pre-existing chat slash commands (/runs, /stop, /careful, /approve, /reject, /tell, /pause, /resume, /help, /exit) return the same output they did before this sprint when dispatched.
npm run build completes with zero TypeScript errors after the hub team registration and the new slash commands are added.

nonGoals: Do not alter the dispatch result of any existing slash command.
Do not route raw sensitive content through chat; the summary lists findings only as already permitted by the source store.
Do not implement the Telegram adapter or its button mapping (owned by telegram-frontend spec).
Do not add new collector, judge, or renderer logic; delegate to the Sprint 2-4 functions.
Do not change the default (non-hub) chat team behaviour.
stopConditions: The hub team is registered, `bober chat hub` selects it, /priority and /decide delegate to the hub judge, existing slash commands are unchanged, and all five success criteria pass evaluation.
npm run build exits zero and the chat hub unit tests plus the existing slash-command tests pass.
definitionOfDone: `bober chat hub` opens a chat session bound to the hub team's memory namespace, where /priority returns a ranked summary of the pooled findings and /decide X vs Y returns the decision-scoped ranking, both delegating to the Sprint 3-4 hub functions and writing priority.md. Every previously existing slash command still returns its prior output, the build is green, and the new tests pass.
assumptions: `bober chat [team]` already selects a team via loadTeam and threads memoryNamespace (src/cli/commands/chat.ts:23-56).
Slash commands are dispatched through src/chat/slash-commands.ts from ChatSession (src/chat/chat-session.ts:200-230); new commands are added additively to that dispatch.
Teams are data not code (team-abstraction memory), so the hub team is a config/registry entry.
The richer Telegram /today|/priority|/decide mapping is owned by the telegram-frontend spec (research:140).
outOfScope: Telegram adapter and inline buttons (telegram-frontend spec).
generatorNotes: Register a hub team as data: follow the team-as-data registry used by loadTeam (src/teams/registry.ts, imported in src/cli/commands/chat.ts:19) and add a hub team with memoryNamespace 'hub' and the default pipelineShape — mirror the example team referenced in the team-abstraction work. `bober chat hub` then resolves through the existing chat command with no command change beyond the team existing. Add /priority and /decide to src/chat/slash-commands.ts dispatch and ChatSession (src/chat/chat-session.ts:200-230): extend the dispatch signature with two new callbacks and add handleHubPriority / handleHubDecide on ChatSession that call the Sprint 2-4 functions (collectFindings -> rankFindings -> renderPriorityMd) and return the ranked summary string. Gate the new commands so they are meaningful only in the hub namespace (e.g. no-op informative message when not the hub team) to avoid coupling other teams. Keep all existing dispatch branches byte-identical. Reuse createClient (src/providers/factory.js) for the judge's LLMClient as elsewhere.
evaluatorNotes: Assert `bober chat hub` constructs a ChatSession whose memoryNamespace equals the hub team's namespace. Drive ChatSession.handleTurn('/priority') with seeded sibling stores and a fake judge client; assert the returned string lists ranks+titles. Drive '/decide X vs Y' and assert decision-scoped output. Re-run the existing slash-command tests (or dispatch each existing command) and assert identical outputs to before. Confirm the new commands do not fire for a non-hub team. Confirm npm run build is green.
